steps.title: Compare to circle form
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation
$$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$
The signs inside the parentheses show the coordinates of the center, and the number on the right is the radius squared.

steps.title: Read center and radius
steps.content: Compare the given equation. $$(x-11)^2+(y-k)^2=64$$ The $x$-coordinate of the center is $11$, the $y$-coordinate is $k$, and $$r=\sqrt{64}=8$$ The answer is A.
